content: Half-life (t1/2) is the time required for plasma drug concentration to decrease by 50% during the elimination phase. It reflects the combined effects of volume of distribution and clearance: t1/2 = (0.693 × Vd) / CL. Most drugs follow first-order kinetics where a constant fraction is eliminated per unit time, making half-life constant regardless of concentration. After one half-life, 50% remains; after two, 25%; after five, ~3% (considered effectively eliminated). Half-life determines dosing frequency and time to steady state (reached after 4-5 half-lives). Drugs with short half-lives require frequent dosing or extended-release formulations; long half-life drugs accumulate and take longer to reach steady state.
